title: Scene-based sensor networks
abstract: An approach to sensor data is based on scenes. One aspect concerns a computer-implemented method for specifying and obtaining a variety of sensor data and processed sensor data related to a scene. The method incorporates a Scene-based API that uses SceneModes and SceneData. An application requesting sensor data communicates a Scene Mode to a group of one or more sensor devices and/or sensor modules via the Scene-based API. The SceneMode determines the SceneData to be captured or provided by the sensor group, which typically includes different types of sensor data related to the Scene and also further processed or analyzed data. The application receives the SceneData from the sensor group via the Scene-based API, with the SceneData organized into SceneShots which are samples of the Scene.
claims:
Claims WHAT IS CLAIMED IS: 1. A computer-implemented method for specifying and obtaining a variety of sensor data and processed sensor data related to a Scene, the method comprising: communicating a SceneMode to a sensor-side technology stack via a Scene-based API, the sensor-side technology stack comprising a group of one or more sensor devices, the SceneMode defining SceneData that is related to the Scene, the SceneData to be provided by the sensor-side technology stack based on a plurality of different types of sensor data captured by the sensor group, the SceneData requiring processing and/or analysis of the captured sensor data; and receiving the SceneData from the sensor-side technology stack via the Scene-based API, the SceneData organized into SceneShots that are samples of the Scene. 2. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the SceneMode determines sensor data to be captured by the sensor group but does not expressly specify sensor-level settings for sensors in the sensor group. 3. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the SceneMode abstracts away from individual sensors in the sensor group. 4. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the SceneMode abstracts away from specific implementation of the required processing and/or analysis. 5. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the SceneMode is one of Home Surveillance, Baby Monitoring, Large Area Surveillance, Personal Assistant, Smart Doorbell, Face Recognition, and Restaurant Camera SceneModes. 6. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the SceneMode is one of Security, Robot, Appliance/IoT (Internet of Things), Health/Lifestyle, Wearables and Leisure SceneModes. 7. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the SceneMode is defined using CaptureModes, and the CaptureModes are building blocks for the SceneModes but are higher level than sensor data captured by sensors in the sensor group. 8. The computer-implemented method of claim 7 wherein the CaptureModes specify sensor data to be captured by the sensors in the sensor group, but abstract away from individual sensors in the sensor group. 9. The computer-implemented method of claim 7 wherein the CaptureModes include at least one of High Dynamic Range, Biomeiric, Kinetic, Multi-focal, Stereo/Array, and Crop CaptureModes. 10. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the Scene Mode is dynamic, and sensor-level settings for the sensor group change as the Scene progresses. 11. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the Scene -based API is a standard. 12. The computer-implemented method of claim 11 wherein the standard includes a set of predefined SceneModes. 13. The computer-implemented method of claim 11 wherein the standard includes a set of predefined SceneModes that can be extended by user-defined SceneModes. 14. The computer-implemented method of claim 1 wherein the SceneData includes CapturedData, which includes at least two types of sensor data captured by the sensor group. 15. The computer-implemented method of claim 14 wherein the CapturedData includes at least two of different types of sensor data selected from the following: image data, audio data, temperature data and ambient environment data. 16. The computer-implemented method of claim 14 wherein the SceneData further includes ProcessedData created by processing the CapturedData. 17. The computer-implemented method of claim 16 wherein the ProcessedData includes at least one of the following: images created by combining two or more different types of captured images, images with depth information, three-dimensional imagery derived from captured images, local images of interest identified by processing captured images, and imagery with motion information. 18. The computer-implemented method of claim 14 wherein the SceneData further includes ResultsOfAnalysisData that is created by analyzing the CapturedData. 19. The computer-implemented method of claim 18 wherein the ResultsOfAnalysisData is a result of analysis based on artificial intelligence or machine learning. 20. The computer-implemented method of claim 18 wherein the ResultsOfAnalysisData is a result of analysis performed by cloud-based services. 21. The computer-implemented method of claim 14 wherein the SceneData further include MetaData for the SceneData. 22. The computer-implemented method of claim 21 wherein the MetaData includes labels for results of processing or analyzing the CapturedData. 23. The computer-implemented method of claim 21 wherein the MetaData includes pointers to additional data relevant to the Scene that is not included in the SceneData. 24. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium storing executable computer program instructions for marking a variety of sensor data and processed sensor data related to 

 a Scene, the instructions executable by a processor and causing the processor to perform any of the methods of claims 1 to 23. 25. A system comprising a processor and a non -transitory computer-readable storage medium storing executable computer program instructions for marking a variety of sensor data and processed sensor data related to a Scene, the instructions executable by a processor and causing the processor to perform any of the methods of claims 1 to 23. 26. A system comprising: an application that consumes sensor data; a sensor-side technology stack comprising a group of one or more sensor devices that capture sensor data; a Scene-based API for communication between the application and the sensor-side technology stack; wherein the application communicates a SceneMode to the sensor-side technology- stack via the Scene-based API, the SceneMode defining SceneData that is related to the Scene, the SceneData to be provided by the sensor-side technology stack based on a plurality of different types of sensor data captured by the sensor group, the SceneData requiring processing and/or analysis of the captured sensor data; and wherein the sensor-side technology stack returns the SceneData to the application via the Scene-based API, the SceneData organized into SceneShots that are samples of the Scene. 27. The system of claim 26 wherein the sensor group consists of one sensor device that is compatible with the Scene-based API, the single sensor device including all sensors required to produce the SceneData, 28. The system of claim 26 wherein the sensor group comprises a plurality of sensor devices, each of which is compatible with the Scene-based API, the plurality of sensor devices in the aggregate including all sensors required to produce the SceneData and coordinating among themselves to capture the sensor data for the SceneData. 29. The system of claim 26 wherein the sensor group comprises a master and a plurality of sensor devices, each sensor device compatible with the Scene -based API, the plurality of sensor devices in the aggregate including all sensors required to produce the SceneData and the master coordinating the sensor devices to capture the sensor data for the SceneData. 30. The system of claim 26 wherein the sensor-side technology stack further comprises middleware that bridges between the Scene-based API and the sensor group. 31. The system of claim 26 wherein one or more applications consume sensor data; the one or more applications communicate a plurality of SceneModes to the sensor-side technology stack via the Scene-based API; and some SceneData is used for at least two SceneModes. 32. The system of claim 26 wherein two or more applications consume sensor data from the same sensors within the sensor group, the applications communicate a plurality of SceneModes to the sensor-side technology stack via the Scene-based API, and some SceneData is returned to two or more applications. 33. A sensor-side technology stack for capturing and producing a variety of sensor data and processed sensor data related to a Scene, the technology stack comprising: a group of one or more sensor devices that in the aggregate capture a plurality of different types of sensor data; a Scene-based API for receiving a SceneMode that is related to the Scene, the SceneData to be provided by the sensor-side technology stack based on the sensor data captured by the sensor group, the SceneData requiring processing and/or analysis of the captured sensor data; and wherein the sensor-side technology stack converts the SceneMode to sensor-level settings for the sensor group, applies the sensor-level settings to the sensor group, carries out the required processing and/or analysis of the captured sensor data, and organizes the captured SceneData into SceneShots that are samples of the Scene. 34. The technology stack of claim 33 wherein the SceneData comprises CapturedData and ProcessedData, the CapturedData including the sensor data captured by the sensor group, the technology stack further comprising: an applications processor that creates the ProcessedData by processing the CapturedData. 35. The technology stack of claim 33 wherein the SceneData comprises CapturedData and ResultsOf Amaiys sData, the CapturedData including the sensor data captured by the sensor group, the technology stack further comprising: an interface to cloud services, the cloud sendees creating the ResultsOfAnalysisData by analy zing the CapturedData. 36. The technology stack of claim 33 further comprising: a low -level sensor interface to at least one of the sensor devices in the sensor group, for providing sensor-level settings to the sensor device: and 

 a middleware module configured io execute the following: receiving the SceneMode from the application via the Scene-based API: converting the SceneMode to corresponding sensor-level settings for the sensor device; transmitting the sensor-level settings to the sensor device via the low-level sensor interface; receiving, via the low-level sensor interface, the sensor data captured by the sensor device according to the sensor-level settings; creating any additional SceneData based on the received sensor data; organizing the sensor data from the sensor device and any additionally created SceneData into SceneShots that are samples of the Scene, according to the SceneMode; and transmitting the SceneData to the application via the Scene-based API. 37. The device of claim 36 wherein the middleware modules comprises at least one of the following: a software platform for the sensor device, a toolkit that provides at least some of the required processing and/or analysis, and a SceneShot formatter for organizing the SceneData into SceneShots. 38. A computer-implemented method for providing intermediation services between applications requesting SceneData and sensor networks capable of providing SceneData, the method comprising: receiving requests for SceneData from a plurality of applications, the SceneData defined by SceneModes specified by the applications via a Scene-based API, the SceneData to be provided by the sensor networks based on a plurality of different types of sensor data captured by the sensor networks, the SceneData requiring processing and/or analysis of the captured sensor data; based on the requested SceneModes, allocating requests for different SceneData among different sensor groups within the sensor networks; receiving the requested SceneData from the sensor groups; and transmitting the SceneData to the applications. 39. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium storing executable computer program instructions for marking a variety of sensor data and processed sensor data related to a Scene, the instructions executable by a processor and causing the processor to perform the method of claim 38. 40. A system comprising a processor and a non-transitory computer-readable storage medium storing executable computer program instructions for marking a variety of sensor data and processed sensor data related to a Scene, the instructions executable by a processor and causing the processor to perform the method of claim 38. 41. A computer-implemented method for specifying sensor data to be captured by a sensor de vice, the method comprising: communicating a CaptureMode to a sensor device via a CaptureMode API, the CaptureMode defining sensor data to be captured by the sensor device but without expressly specifying sensor-level settings for the sensor device; and receiving the sensor data from the sensor device. 42. The computer-implemented method of claim 41 wherein the CaptureMode abstracts away from specific implementation of the sensor device. 43. The computer-implemented method of claim 41 wherein the CaptureMode include at least one of High Dynamic Range, Biometric, Kinetic, Multi-focal, Stereo/Array, and Crop CaptureModes. 44. The computer-implemented method of claim 41 wherein the CaptureMode also defines a second type of sensor data to be captured by a second sensor device but without expressly specifying sensor-level settings for the second sensor device. 45. The computer-implemented method of claim 41 wherein the CaptureMode API is a standard. 46. The computer-implemented method of claim 45 wherein the standard includes a set of predefined CaptureModes. 47. The computer-implemented method of claim 45 wherein the standard includes a set of predefined CaptureModes that can be extended by user-defined CaptureModes. 48. The computer-implemented method of claim 41 wherein the CaptureMode further defines processed data to be provided, the processed data based on processing the captured sensor data. 49. The computer-implemented method of claim 41 wherein the CaptureMode further defines meta data to be provided by the sensor device. 50. The computer-implemented method of claim 49 wherein the meta data includes labels for results of processing the captured sensor data. 51. A non-transitory computer-readable storage medium storing executable computer program instructions for marking a variety of sensor data and processed sensor data related to 

 a Scene, the instructions executable by a processor and causing the processor to perform any of the methods of claims 41 to 50. 52, A system comprising a processor and a non -transitory computer-readable storage medium storing executable computer program instructions for marking a variety of sensor data and processed sensor data related to a Scene, the instructions executable by a processor and causing the processor to perform any of the methods of claims 41 to 50.
